bullets.text: Combined ratio was just below 90 for Q1 2009, producing $356 million in pretax underwriting profit; performance was widely distributed by product and state, meeting the critical imperative to rebuild capital.

Progressive recognized an additional $223 million in other-than-temporary impairments on bank preferred stocks; the investment portfolio was actively de-risked with increased allocation to cash and U.S. Treasury bonds.
Auto written premium grew 3% and policies in force grew 3% year-over-year; Agency auto contributed -1% and -2% respectively, while Direct auto contributed 9% and 10%.
Significant increase in new prospects and sales, with Agency new business at run rates similar to a year ago after extended declines, and Direct Internet production up strongly year-over-year.
Capital position strengthened by ~$90 million during Q1 and ~$175 million sequentially over February; remained several hundred million above regulatory plus extreme contingency capital levels.
Severity trends were relatively mild except for personal injury protection in no-fault states; claims frequency was lower year-over-year but slowly climbing from Q3 2008 lows.

Direct channel, especially Internet production, showed increases counter to general economic lethargy; Agency new auto business returned to prior-year run rates after twelve months of significant declines.
Name Your Price was about to go national after nearly a year of promising rollout; management cited increased consumer cost-consciousness as a meaningful driver of shopping and switching behavior.
Advertising campaign continued building brand momentum and credibility; management noted consumer research shows Progressive is perceived as both credible and good value during economic stress.
